1: direct The LORD reigns; he is robed in majesty; the LORD is robed; he has put on strength as his belt. Yes, the world is established; it shall never be moved. The LORD who reigns is Christ enthroned: all authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me (Matthew 28:18). Revelation applies this throne imagery to Christ throughout (Rev 4-5; Psalms 19:6 — the Lord our God the Almighty reigns). His majesty is the Ascension glory; his belt of strength is the power of the resurrection.
2: direct Your throne is established from of old; you are from everlasting. The eternal throne — Hebrews 1:8 applies Psalms 45:6 to Christ: Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The one from everlasting is the Christ who pre-exists all creation (John 1:1 — in the beginning was the Word; Colossians 1:17 — he is before all things). The ancient throne is the throne of the Son of God.
3: shadow The floods have lifted up, O LORD, the floods have lifted up their voice; the floods lift up their roaring. Roaring floods — the chaos waters that threaten. Christ commands the chaos: Peace! Be still! (Mark 4:39) — the disciples ask, Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him? The one mightier than the roaring floods is the LORD of Ps 93, present in the boat.
4: shadow Mightier than the thunders of many waters, mightier than the waves of the sea, the LORD on high is mighty! Mightier than the waves — the LORD who stills the storm is Christ. His voice of authority over nature (Matthew 8:26; Mark 4:39; Luke 8:24) demonstrates his identity as the creator-God who is mightier than every force of chaos. The voice like many waters in Revelation 1:15 is the voice of the risen Christ.
5: shadow Your decrees are very trustworthy; holiness befits your house, O LORD, forevermore. Holiness befitting God's house — Christ cleanses the temple as an act of divine holiness (John 2:13-17). But he is himself the new house where holiness dwells: for in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily (Colossians 2:9). The trustworthy decrees are the words of Christ: heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away (Matthew 24:35).
